description:
## What's Wrong
`just release-patch` / `just release-minor` use `sed -i` in a way that's GNU-sed specific; BSD sed (macOS outside nix) requires an extension argument.

## Where
- justfile:67-68, 87-88

## Why It Matters
Contributors running release scripts on macOS without nix will hit a broken release flow.

## Suggested Fix
- Use a portable approach: `perl -pi -e`, or detect BSD vs GNU sed.
- Consider moving release logic to `scripts/release.sh` with explicit checks.

## Acceptance
- Release scripts work on macOS + Linux without requiring GNU sed.
title: Release scripts: make version bump portable across macOS/Linux

closed_reason: PR review fix: exact WAL size precheck now accounts for persisted durability claims before append
description: Durability wait polling recomputes eligibility dynamically, allowing one request to observe moving quorum semantics. Capture cohort/epoch snapshot in request claim and evaluate against fixed cohort to prevent flapping correctness.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Audit note:
- Workspace: /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs
- jj change: zzkxnuyx / b127c0a7
- Scope: froze replicated durability cohorts via persisted DurabilityRequestClaim across request handling, WAL header/index persistence, replay, waiter polling, and idempotent retry reuse.
- Legacy rows without stored claims now take one current claim snapshot before polling so a single retry cannot observe moving quorum semantics.
- Compatibility: bumped WAL format version to 3 and updated current-version fixtures/tests accordingly.
- Added regressions for persisted-claim retry reuse, legacy no-claim retry freezing, WAL record claim round-trip, WAL contract/index round-trip, and stateright model call-site propagation.
- Verification passed: cargo fmt --all --check; just dylint; cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings; cargo test; cargo xtest; cargo test -p beads-daemon --features test-harness; cargo test -p beads-daemon-core --all-features.
- Adversarial review: two independent review passes found (1) missing WAL format bump and (2) legacy retry two-snapshot drift / missing coverage; both were fixed, verification rerun, and final reruns came back clean.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Audit note:
- Workspace: /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs
- jj change: xrvuwyul / 64facd07
- Scope: fixed the follow-up PR review gap by prechecking exact encoded WAL record size for client requests with persisted durability claims before any WAL append attempt.
- Root cause: planner-side record-size estimation did not account for the persisted durability claim bytes added in bd-p0n8, so oversize replicated client requests could fail late as EventWal::RecordTooLarge instead of the daemon's WalRecordTooLarge surface.
- Implementation: added exact encoded-length helpers on WAL records, used that exact length in the executor before wal_append, and added an executor regression that proves both the estimate gap and the pre-append boundary by asserting no active WAL segment and no indexed segment rows are created on rejection.
- Verification passed: cargo test -p beads-daemon --lib replicated_client_request_prechecks_exact_wal_record_size -- --nocapture; cargo test -p beads-daemon --lib idempotent_retry_ -- --nocapture; cargo test -p beads-daemon-core -- --nocapture; cargo test -p beads-daemon --features test-harness; just dylint; cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings; cargo fmt --all --check.
- Adversarial review: Einstein found one valid proof-gap in the first regression draft (error shape without proving pre-append rejection); fixed by asserting no segment/index side effects; final Einstein and Huygens reruns came back clean.

title: P0: Freeze durability wait cohort/epoch for deterministic request outcomes
